brief_title: Psilocybin-Assisted Therapy for Major Depressive Disorder
official_title: A Phase 2, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study of Psilocybin-Assisted Therapy in Adults with Moderate-to-Severe Major Depressive Disorder Without Psychotic Features
interventions: Psilocybin 25mg single oral dose with psychological support (two sessions, 3 weeks apart)
brief_summary: This Phase 2 study evaluates the efficacy and safety of two psilocybin-assisted therapy sessions, 3 weeks apart, compared to placebo with identical psychological support in adults with moderate-to-severe major depressive disorder. The primary endpoint is change in MADRS total score at week 6.
raw_inclusion_text:
1. Adults aged 25-65 years

2. DSM-5 diagnosis of major depressive disorder without psychotic features

3. Current depressive episode duration >= 3 months

4. PHQ-9 score >= 15 at screening (moderate-to-severe depression)

5. MADRS total score >= 20 at screening

6. Either treatment-naive or has tried at least one antidepressant medication

7. If on antidepressant, must complete washout period before dosing session

8. Medically healthy based on physical exam, ECG, and laboratory tests

9. Able to attend all preparatory, dosing, and integration therapy sessions

10. Written informed consent

11. Agreement not to use recreational psychedelics during the study

12. Reliable emergency contact available
raw_exclusion_text:
1. Current or lifetime psychotic disorder (schizophrenia, schizoaffective disorder)

2. First-degree relative with psychotic disorder

3. Bipolar disorder (type I or II)

4. Current PTSD, OCD, or personality disorder as primary diagnosis

5. Active suicidal ideation with specific plan or recent attempt (within 12 months)

6. Substance use disorder within past 12 months (excluding nicotine or caffeine)

7. Uncontrolled seizure disorder

8. Cardiovascular disease (uncontrolled hypertension, recent MI, arrhythmia)

9. Pregnant or breastfeeding

10. Prior adverse reaction to a psychedelic substance

inclusion_criteria.text: DSM-5 diagnosis of major depressive disorder without psychotic features
Either treatment-naive or has tried at least one antidepressant medication
If on antidepressant, must complete washout period before dosing session
Medically healthy based on physical exam, ECG, and laboratory tests

Agreement not to use recreational psychedelics during the study

exclusion_criteria.text: Current or lifetime psychotic disorder (schizophrenia, schizoaffective disorder)
Current PTSD, OCD, or personality disorder as primary diagnosis
Active suicidal ideation with specific plan or recent attempt (within 12 months)
Substance use disorder within past 12 months (excluding nicotine or caffeine)
Cardiovascular disease (uncontrolled hypertension, recent MI, arrhythmia)
